Mapping from Health System Challenges to Services and Application Types


Group 1 Mapping from Health System Challenges to Services and Application Types

Source CodeRelationshipTarget CodeComment
Ais equivalent toC1v1 'Census; population information & data warehouse' → v2 C1 'Census and population information systems'
Bis equivalent toC2v1 'Civil Registration and Vital Statistics' → v2 C2 [same concept; refined name]
Cmaps loosely toA7v1 'Client applications' [broad] most closely maps to v2 A7 'Personal health records'; point-of-service apps generally fall under A. group
Dis equivalent toA1v1 'Client communication system' → v2 A1 'Communication systems'

Pis equivalent toC6v1 'Identification registries and directories' → v2 C6 [same concept]
Qis equivalent toD7v1 'Knowledge Management' → v2 D7 'Knowledge management systems'
Ris equivalent toA6v1 'Laboratory and Diagnostic System' → v2 A6 'Laboratory information systems'
Sis equivalent toB5v1 'Learning and Training System' → v2 B5 'Learning and training systems'
Tis equivalent toB6v1 'Logistics Management Information System' → v2 B6 'Logistics management information systems [LMIS]'
Uis equivalent toA8v1 'Pharmacy Information System' → v2 A8 'Pharmacy information systems'
Vis equivalent toE2v1 'Public health and disease surveillance' → v2 E2 'Public health and disease surveillance systems'
Wis equivalent toB8v1 'Research information system' → v2 B8 'Research information systems'
Xis equivalent toD8v1 'SHR and health information repositories' → v2 D8 'Shared Health Record and Health Information Repository'
Yis equivalent toA9v1 'Telemedicine' → v2 A9 'Telehealth systems'

name: CDSCv1toCDSCv2
title: Mapping from CDSC v1 to Services and Application Types v2
description:
Mapping from the Classification of Digital Health System Categories v1 (CDSCv1, 2018)
to the Classification of Digital Health Services and Application Types v2 (CDSCv2, 2023).

The v1 used 25 single-letter codes (A–Y). The v2 completely restructured this into
5 representations within the digital health enterprise architecture, each with
alphanumeric codes (A1–A9, B1–B8, C1–C11, D1–D8, E1–E2).

Several new v2 categories have no v1 equivalent:
A3 (Decision support), A4 (Diagnostics), B1 (Blood bank), B3 (Health program monitoring),
B7 (Patient administration), C4 (Facility registries), C5 (Health worker registry),
C7 (Immunisation information), C8 (Master patient index), C9 (Product catalogues),
C10 (Public Key directories), D1 (Analytics), D3 (Data warehouses).
